reasoning: AOS is scheduled to report Q3 2025 earnings on October 28, 2025, just after our knowledge cutoff of October 26, 2025. Earnings reactions are highly volatile and largely symmetric in expectation unless there is strong prior guidance. The stock price recently pulled back from ~72 to ~68 just before the earnings release. Since stock prices generally exhibit a long-term upward drift (the equity risk premium) and individual stock returns over short-to-medium horizons are essentially unpredictable noise around this drift, I will apply base rates. The probability of a single stock being higher than today's price is approximately 51-52% over one week, drifting slightly higher as the time horizon extends to one month and three months. I will set the probabilities at 0.52 for the earliest resolution date, grading smoothly to 0.55 for the farthest out date to reflect standard equity drift.
